methods.method_name: Random Forest Modeling for Pore Characteristics
methods.description: Random forest models were used to build predictive models and identify the major determinants for each pore characteristic (porosity, pore density, and pore size) using the rpart, randomForest, and party packages in R. This method is robust to colinearity, nonlinearity, and deviations from normality in the data, and is useful for data sets with some missing data.

steps.description: Random forest models were used to build predictive models and identify the major determinants for each pore characteristic using the rpart, randomForest, and party packages in R.
steps.searchable_summary: build random forest models using rpart, randomForest, and party packages in R

steps.description: The strength of each predictor variable is assessed by the reduction in model fit when that variable is excluded.
steps.searchable_summary: assess predictor variable strength by reduction in model fit

steps.description: An unbiased, conditional variable importance ranking method was incorporated via the party package in R.
steps.searchable_summary: use party package in R for conditional variable importance ranking
